SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.depressurization

Class DynamicPsvSizingStudy

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.depressurization.DynamicPsvSizingStudy
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class DynamicPsvSizingStudy extends Object implements Serializable
Dynamic pressure-safety-valve (PSV) sizing study for a fire-exposed gas-filled vessel.

Steady-state API STD 521 fire sizing assumes the relief device passes the peak fire vapour-generation rate continuously at the accumulated relieving pressure. In reality a spring-loaded PSV on a gas system cycles (pops open at the set pressure, reseats at the blowdown pressure), and the vessel inventory and the fire heat input both change with time. A transient simulation that resolves this PSV cycling typically shows that a smaller orifice keeps the peak accumulated pressure within the allowable limit than the steady-state equation requires, i.e. the steady-state method is conservative. This study quantifies that conservatism as an oversizing ratio.

The study couples a lumped real-gas vessel energy and mass balance, a cycling PsvValveModel, and a constant fire heat input. The dynamically required orifice area is found by bisection as the smallest area that keeps the peak vessel pressure at or below the allowable accumulated pressure. It is then compared against the steady-state API 521 area from ReliefValveSizing.calculateRequiredArea(double, double, double, double, double, double, double, double, boolean, boolean).

This is a screening-grade comparison: the cycling dynamics use ideal-gas energy bookkeeping seeded from a single NeqSim flash for molar mass, heat-capacity ratio and compressibility.

References: API STD 521 7th ed. §4.4 and §5.15; Speirs and Kirby PSV dynamic-sizing studies.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • DynamicPsvSizingStudy

      public DynamicPsvSizingStudy(SystemInterface fluid, double vesselVolume, double fireHeatInputW, double setPressurePa, double overpressureFraction, double backPressurePa)
      Constructs a dynamic PSV sizing study.
      Parameters:
      fluid - gas fluid in the vessel; mixing rule must already be set
      vesselVolume - internal vessel volume in m³; must be positive
      fireHeatInputW - fire heat input to the fluid in W; must be positive
      setPressurePa - PSV set pressure in Pa absolute; must be positive
      overpressureFraction - allowable accumulation fraction (0.21 for fire); must be positive
      backPressurePa - downstream absolute pressure in Pa; must be positive
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fluid is null or an argument is not positive

  • Method Details

    • setBlowdownFraction

      public DynamicPsvSizingStudy setBlowdownFraction(double blowdownFraction)
      Sets the PSV blowdown fraction (reseat margin below the set pressure).
      Parameters:
      blowdownFraction - blowdown fraction in (0, 1)
      Returns:
      this study for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if out of range

    • setDischargeCoefficient

      public DynamicPsvSizingStudy setDischargeCoefficient(double dischargeCoefficient)
      Sets the PSV discharge coefficient.
      Parameters:
      dischargeCoefficient - discharge coefficient; must be positive
      Returns:
      this study for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if not positive

    • setLatentHeat

      public DynamicPsvSizingStudy setLatentHeat(double latentHeat)
      Sets the latent heat of vaporization used for the steady-state fire relieving rate.
      Parameters:
      latentHeat - latent heat in J/kg; must be positive
      Returns:
      this study for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if not positive

    • setTimeStep

      public DynamicPsvSizingStudy setTimeStep(double dt)
      Sets the transient integration time step.
      Parameters:
      dt - time step in s; must be positive
      Returns:
      this study for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if not positive

    • setMaxTime

      public DynamicPsvSizingStudy setMaxTime(double tMax)
      Sets the maximum simulation time.
      Parameters:
      tMax - maximum time in s; must be positive
      Returns:
      this study for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if not positive

    • run

      public DynamicPsvSizingStudy.SizingComparison run()
      Runs the steady-versus-dynamic sizing comparison.
      Returns:
      a DynamicPsvSizingStudy.SizingComparison with the steady-state and dynamic required orifice areas and the oversizing ratio

    • simulatePeakPressure

      private double simulatePeakPressure(double orificeAreaM2, double initialTempK, double mw, double gamma, double z)
      Simulates the PSV-cycling fire transient for a candidate orifice and returns the peak vessel pressure.
      Parameters:
      orificeAreaM2 - candidate PSV orifice area in m²
      initialTempK - initial gas temperature in K
      mw - molar mass in kg/mol
      gamma - heat-capacity ratio
      z - compressibility factor
      Returns:
      peak vessel pressure in Pa over the transient
